Video.summary: Debbie is in for a shocking surprise when she catches her boyfriend, Terrance, sneaking around with her twin sister.
Video.title: Young vs. Goodwin

Video.summary: Plaintiff, Reprudentia, is suing a fellow Cameroonian countryman for the $5000 she gave him to buy her a car.
Video.title: Car Troubles

Video.summary: After calling off their wedding, Smilez sues Dejsha for crashing his music video and breaking his $12,000 chain.
Video.title: Harrell vs. Watson

Video.summary: Tavares Brown sues David Thompson for $5,000, claiming false accusations of pulling a gun got him arrested and cost him money. Thompson countersues for $5,000, insisting Brown threatened him with a gun.
Video.title: You Lied to the Police and Got Me Arrested!

Image.alt: You Lied to the Police and Got Me Arrested!
You Lied to the Police and Got Me Arrested!
You Lied to the Police and Got Me Arrested!

Video.summary: McKenzie is suing her baby's father for the price of a car because he chooses work and football over family.
Video.title: Football Over Family

Video.summary: Heather is suing her mother. After she bred her dog, her mother gave two of the puppies to her sister to sell.
Video.title: Dog Tired

Video.summary: A man seeks justice for stolen property and unpaid debt while questioning his future with a distant girlfriend.
Video.title: Whitehurst vs. Briggs

Video.summary: A woman sues her boyfriend after he tore her dress saying it was too revealing.
Video.title: Edmonds vs. Steele

Video.summary: After a life-altering amputation, Brooke brings girlfriend Brenna to court to address concerns regarding her caretaking.
Video.title: Fletcher vs. Stewart

Video.summary: It's music biz mayhem when two men tangle over their boy band. Was it a record label rip off?
Video.title: The Reunion of Swag Kids

Video.summary: Linda says John wrote an IOU for his DUI but he said he was drunk during signing.
Video.title: Too Sauced to Sign

Video.summary: BFFs Earl and Demoun brawl over stolen sushi. But when Demoun put away his fist, he took out his hammer!
Video.title: Fish and Hammer

Video.summary: Monique and Joshua sue landlord Brandi for withholding their $350 deposit, claiming control issues and unfair treatment. Brandi counters, saying Monique’s son broke rules, caused trouble, and left the property damaged.
Video.title: Your House Is Haunted by a Ghost!

Image.alt: Your House Is Haunted by a Ghost!
Your House Is Haunted by a Ghost!
Your House Is Haunted by a Ghost!

Video.summary: Plaintiff Tien sues landlord Jeffrey for $2,450, claiming he broke his promise to refund her after she backed out of renting his house. Jeffrey insists he doesn’t owe more, saying he already refunded most of the money and was left covering costs.
Video.title: Rental Ruckus

Video.summary: Olivia is suing her best friend's daughter over a utility bill but Kalia wants nothing to do with her.
Video.title: Someone’s Full of Hot Air

Video.summary: The plaintiff, who took guardianship of the defendant's baby because she was born with meth in her system, sues the birth mother for breach of contract; the defendant counter-sues for unreturned property and says the plaintiff is vindictive.
Video.title: Guardianship Grudges

Video.summary: Damage to personal items, theft of a dog, and unpaid rent have exes fighting.
Video.title: Bey vs. Williams

Video.summary: Antonio Lopez and Yajaira Rodriguez sue wedding planner Maria Martinez for $5,000, claiming she did nothing and stole their money. Maria says Yajaira canceled meetings, and a couple’s fight caused chaos, leaving her stuck in the middle.
Video.title: Wedding Disaster

Video.summary: WENET and the SED demonstrate a large scale marijuana bust near an elementary school.
Video.title: Mexican Cartel Pot Grow in Our Backyard

Image.alt: Mexican Cartel Pot Grow in Our Backyard
Mexican Cartel Pot Grow in Our Backyard
Mexican Cartel Pot Grow in Our Backyard

Video.summary: WENET plans and executes a large Methamphetamine cartel raid.
Video.title: Mexican Cartel Meth House

Video.summary: Anastasia is a hair stylist here to sue one of her clients for an unpaid loan and emotional distress.
Video.title: Wigging Out Over an Unpaid Loan

Image.alt: Wigging Out Over an Unpaid Loan
Wigging Out Over an Unpaid Loan
Wigging Out Over an Unpaid Loan

Video.summary: Paulita is suing her baby daddy for the value of a car, saying she left him because he's an abusive drunk.
Video.title: Paying Up for the Car

Video.summary: Terrell sues girlfriend Kristy for damages because he suspects she is using him for money.
Video.title: Marrow vs. Coleman

Video.summary: Tandrea sues her ex-girlfriend, Chantay, for $1,000 in pet fees that she failed to pay when they moved into an apartment together.
Video.title: Davis vs. Goodman

Video.summary: An entertainer seeks help from the judges on how to deal with his girlfriend’s insecurity surrounding his line of work.
Video.title: Obiowo vs. Vranas

Video.summary: A marriage is on the line as accusations of betrayal and overprotection threaten two decades of love and business.
Video.title: Heard vs. Macon

Video.summary: Demario and Necole’s relationship is tested when he tries to impose his conservative Christian values onto her.
Video.title: Scott vs. Thomas

Video.summary: When the Cowans find they’re growing apart after 10 years of marriage, they come before the judges for help.
Video.title: Cowan vs Cowan

Video.summary: Cherry Smith sues neighbor Mitchell Martinez for $3,000, claiming his loud behavior damaged her mirror and door. Mitchell denies the accusations, calling Cherry a long-time complainer. He countersues for $1,000, alleging harassment and missed work.
Video.title: Neighbor Nightmare

Video.summary: Keith Groubert sues his brother Jonathon for $5,000, claiming he stole a valuable Marc Chagall painting. Jonathon denies it, saying the painting is a fake and accuses Keith of stealing family jewelry.
Video.title: Stealing From a Brother

Video.summary: Plaintiff Mumena Boru accuses her ex, Angelo Woodard, of breaking into her apartment, stealing her TV, and taking her phone. Angelo claims the TV is his and countersues for $5,000, alleging wasted time and emotional distress.
Video.title: You Stole My Kids TV

Video.summary: Muharrem and Milos sue Peter for $5,000, claiming he wrongfully towed their car without police orders, causing lost wages. Peter countersues for $700, alleging the car had bogus plates and was on the street illegally.
Video.title: Tussle Over a Tow

Video.summary: A photographer says the defendants told her their wedding ceremony was canceled due to the pandemic, so she refunded their money; she now believes that was a lie, so she's suing.
Video.title: Save the Fake Date

Video.summary: Delores says her twin sister agreed to split her medical bills when she became ill while planning her party.
Video.title: Bachelorette Party Gone Bad

Video.summary: Tajanea is suing her ex-boyfriend for over $2,000 after she claimed he damaged her car and apartment.
Video.title: Fumbling with the Keys and Relationship

Image.alt: Fumbling with the Keys and Relationship
Fumbling with the Keys and Relationship
Fumbling with the Keys and Relationship

Video.summary: Amanda is suing her ex-best friend Bernardo for thousands spent on bail money to hair care.
Video.title: Ya Booze Ya Lose

Video.summary: Dawn wants her unpaid wages. Stegen says the organizers didn't pay him so he wasn't obligated to pay her.
Video.title: The Exotic Erotic Ball

Video.summary: Did their office friendship go too far? Are her misconduct claims a cover for the money she owes?
Video.title: Gross Misconduct

Video.summary: Daryl is suing his ex-friend for missing belongings that he believes were sold to fuel a gambling addiction.
Video.title: Gambling the Friendship

Video.summary: Arleen says she generously helped her ex pay his rent, get around town, and that she kept him looking good. Marcus denies it all, claiming her paid all the rent and never took a dime from her.
Video.title: We Can't Make Up Until You Pay for the Makeup!

Image.alt: We Can't Make Up Until You Pay for the Makeup!
We Can't Make Up Until You Pay for the Makeup!
We Can't Make Up Until You Pay for the Makeup!

Video.summary: They were old time band buddies that had a rockin new idea, then they say an old band buddy lured them in with a sweet song, but when his tune fell flat, they fell between a Rock N' Roll and a hard place.
Video.title: Band Buddy Battle!

Video.summary: The SED act on a felony warrant. Deputy Miller breaks up an underage party in the woods.
Video.title: Smile, You're on My Surveillance System!

Image.alt: Smile, You're on My Surveillance System!
Smile, You're on My Surveillance System!
Smile, You're on My Surveillance System!

Video.summary: A troubled 15-year-old boy goes through the booking process.
Video.title: Scared Straight? We Hope So!

Video.summary: SWAT carries out a mission to arrest a man wanted for assault with a deadly weapon.
Video.title: SWAT vs. Dirty Harry

Video.summary: Deputy Turnbull makes a visit to a known flop house.
Video.title: Dopers Come Together
